name: Sesame Candy
recipeInstructions:
-------------
1. Do not make candy on a hot or humid day.
2. In a deep, medium-size saucepan, slowly heat honey over medium-low heat until it reaches the hard-crack stage (about 305°F to 310°F on a candy thermometer). Stir occasionally to prevent boiling over for about 45 minutes.
3. Stir in sesame seeds and set aside for a few minutes to cool slightly.
4. Grease a 9 x 13 inch baking sheet with peanut oil. Do not use waxed paper.
5. Pour the honey mixture onto the greased baking sheet.
6. Set the baking sheet on a cooling rack until cooled, but not hard.
7. Score the candy into diamond shapes once it has cooled completely.
8. When fully cooled, remove the candy and separate into pieces.
This recipe is listed as a Saudi Arabian recipe from Frugal Gourmet, "On Our Immigrant Ancestors" by Jeff Smith, p. 479.
